content:
Member-only story

5 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

The most dangerous update bug is quiet.
A user opens an edit screen. Another user changes the same record. The first user clicks save. Without concurrency control, the last write wins and the earlier change disappears. No exception. No warning. Just lost data.
EF Core optimistic concurrency and HTTP ETags give ASP.NET Core APIs a clean way to prevent this. The database detects stale writes. The API communicates version state to clients. The client retries with intent instead of accidentally overwriting someone else’s work.
The architecture
We will build:

• An EF Core entity with a row version concurrency token.

• A GET endpoint that returns an ETag.

• A PUT endpoint that requires If-Match.

• Conflict handling for stale updates.

• Problem Details responses clients can understand.
Product entity
The RowVersion property is the concurrency token. SQL Server can generate it automatically with rowversion.
public sealed class Product
{
public Guid Id { get; private set; }
public string Sku { get; private set; } = string.Empty…
